get_application.construction_type: Interior non-structural alterations, mechanical upgrades for commercial kitchen ventilation

get_parcel_data.existing_improvements: Two-story commercial building constructed 1987. No historic designation.

get_sensitive_area_overlay.flood_zone: Zone X — Area of minimal flood hazard. Outside 500-year floodplain. FIRM Panel 06071C0823G.
get_sensitive_area_overlay.wetland_buffer: None — parcel not within 100 ft of any mapped wetland or waterway.
get_sensitive_area_overlay.biological_resources: Not within a mapped biological resources survey area or MSHCP criteria cell.
get_sensitive_area_overlay.cultural_resources: Low sensitivity area per General Plan Figure CR-1. No known archaeological resources within 500 ft.
get_sensitive_area_overlay.seismic_hazard: Zone C — No Alquist-Priolo fault zone. Standard CBC seismic design applies.
get_sensitive_area_overlay.wildfire_hazard: Not within a State Responsibility Area or Very High Fire Hazard Severity Zone.

get_ceqa_exemption_text.class_1_text: Class 1 consists of the operation, repair, maintenance, permitting, leasing, licensing, or minor alteration of existing public or private structures, facilities, mechanical equipment, or topographical features, involving negligible or no expansion of existing or former use. The types of 'existing facilities' itemized below are not intended to be all-inclusive of the types of projects that might fall within Class 1. The key consideration is whether the project involves negligible or no expansion of an existing use.
get_ceqa_exemption_text.class_1_subsection_b: §15301(b): Existing facilities of both investor and publicly-owned utilities used to provide electric power, natural gas, sewerage, or other public utility services.
get_ceqa_exemption_text.class_1_subsection_e: §15301(e): Additions to existing structures provided that the addition will not result in an increase of more than: (1) 50 percent of the floor area of the structures before the addition, or (2) 2,500 sq ft, whichever is less.

section_15300_2_exceptions.text: 14 CCR §15300.2 — Exceptions. A categorical exemption shall not be used for a project which may cause a significant effect on the environment due to unusual circumstances. In addition to this general exception, the specific exceptions set forth in this section apply to certain categorically exempt classes.
section_15300_2_exceptions.subsection_a: §15300.2(a): Location. Classes 3, 4, 5, 6, and 11 are not applicable to a project located in a particularly sensitive environment where the project may impact on an environmental resource of hazardous or critical concern where designated, precisely mapped, and officially adopted pursuant to law by federal, state, or local agencies.
section_15300_2_exceptions.subsection_b: §15300.2(b): Cumulative Impact. All exemptions for these classes are inapplicable when the cumulative impact of successive projects of the same type in the same place, over time is significant.
section_15300_2_exceptions.subsection_c: §15300.2(c): Significant Effect. A categorical exemption shall not be used for a project which may cause a significant effect on the environment due to unusual circumstances.
section_15300_2_exceptions.note: §15300.2 exceptions apply to specified classes. Class 1 is subject to the general significant-effect exception at §15300.2(c) but not to the location exception at §15300.2(a).

get_municipal_code.permitted_uses: Eating and drinking establishments are a permitted use in the C-2 zone (Municipal Code §18.24.020(A)(8)). No conditional use permit required for restaurants under 5,000 sq ft.
get_municipal_code.development_standards: No change to building footprint, height, setbacks, or parking. Existing parking supply of 24 spaces meets the parking standard for restaurant use (1 space per 100 sq ft = 38 spaces required). Variance or adjustment would be required but applicant has recorded shared parking agreement with adjacent lot (14 additional spaces) on file.
get_municipal_code.note: Change of use from retail to restaurant in C-2 zone is a by-right permitted use. No discretionary approval required for the land use component.
